title: FIXFlux - Decision & Compliance Logs
description: Saved LogQL queries for pre-trade risk decisions, compliance violations, cross-service order tracing, and system-wide error surfacing

panels.title: All Pre-trade Decisions
panels.description:
Every order evaluation logged by the risk service — both approvals and rejections.

Look for the `decision=APPROVED` or `decision=REJECTED` field, along with `notional`, `last_price`, and `reason` to understand why each decision was made.

LogQL: `{container=~".*risk-service.*"} |= "pre_trade_decision"`

panels.title: Rejected Orders Only
panels.description:
Orders blocked by the risk service before reaching the matching engine.

Filtered to WARNING level logs containing `decision=REJECTED`. The `reason` field explains which rule fired (e.g. notional cap exceeded, fat-finger threshold, position limit).

LogQL: `{container=~".*risk-service.*"} | level="WARNING" |= "decision=REJECTED"`

panels.title: Compliance Violations Detected
panels.description:
Orders flagged by the compliance service. Defaults to showing all violations — narrow to a specific rule by appending `|= "RuleName"` to the query (e.g. `|= "TradeSizeRule"`, `|= "WashTradingRule"`, `|= "DuplicateOrderRule"`).

The `violations` field shows the count and `rules` lists which rules fired.

LogQL: `{container=~".*compliance-consumer.*"} |= "VIOLATIONS_DETECTED"`

panels.title: Order Trace (replace ORDER_ID in query)
panels.description:
Follows a single order ID across both the risk service and compliance consumer — useful for understanding the full lifecycle of one specific order.

Before using: click the query editor icon (pencil) and replace `<ORDER_ID>` with the actual order ID from your logs (e.g. `ORD-abc123`).

LogQL: `{container=~".*(risk-service|compliance-consumer).*"} |= "order=<ORDER_ID>"`

panels.title: All WARNING / ERROR Logs (system-wide)
panels.description:
Surfaces WARNING and ERROR level logs from every container in the stack — a fast way to spot problems without knowing which service to look at first.

Filtered to `stderr` stream, which is where Python logging writes WARNING and above by default.

LogQL: `{logstream="stderr"} | level=~"WARNING|ERROR"`
